sentences.base_language: I work as a software engineer.
sentences.grammarNote: كـ (ka-) as a prefix means 'as' or 'like'. كمهندس = as an engineer. This prefix attaches directly to the noun.

sentences.base_language: I have been learning Arabic for six months.
sentences.grammarNote: منذ (mundhu) = since / for (with duration). Used with the present tense in Arabic to describe ongoing actions that started in the past, unlike English which uses the present perfect.

sentences.base_language: Arabic is difficult but I enjoy it.
My favorite part is learning new words.
sentences.target_language: الجزء المفضل لدي هو تعلم الكلمات الجديدة.
sentences.romanized: al-juz' al-mufaddal ladayya huwa ta'allum al-kalimat al-jadida.
sentences.literal: The-part the-favorite to-me is learning the-words the-new.

sentences.base_language: I practice every day for thirty minutes.
sentences.target_language: أتدرب كل يوم لمدة ثلاثين دقيقة.
sentences.romanized: atadarrab kull yawm li-mudat thalathin daqiqa.
sentences.literal: I-practice every day for-duration thirty minute.

sentences.base_language: I also listen to Arabic music.
One day I want to visit Egypt and Jordan.
sentences.target_language: يوماً ما أريد زيارة مصر والأردن.
sentences.romanized: yawman ma urid ziyarat misr wal-urdunn.

sentences.base_language: I hope to become fluent one day.
sentences.target_language: آمل أن أصبح طليقاً يوماً ما.
sentences.romanized: amal an asbah taliqan yawman ma.
sentences.literal: I-hope that I-become fluent day some.
